title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 9 (Fragment 3)
text:
“This is a dream!” Tonino told himself. But he knew it was not. He had a fair idea what was happening, though his head still felt muzzy and his eyes were blurred. As he raced back down the little platform, he turned his bleary eyes inwards, towards the heaviness on his face. Sure enough, blurred and doubled, he could see a great red and pink nose there. He was Mr. Punch.

Naturally, then, he tried to dig in his heels and stop racing up and down, and to lift his hand and wrench off the huge pink nose. He could not do either. More than that, whoever was making him be Mr. Punch promptly took mean pleasure in making him run faster and whirl the sausages about harder.

“Oh, very good!” yelled someone from the lighted space.

Tonino thought he knew that voice. He sped towards the cardboard Dog Toby again, whirled the sausages away from its cardboard jaws and waited for his head and eyes to stop feeling so fuzzy. He was sure they would. The mean person wanted him conscious. “What a clever fellow!” he squawked. As he raced down the stage the other way, he snatched a look across his huge nose towards the lighted space, but it was a blur. So he snatched a look towards the other side.

He saw the wall of a golden villa there, with four long windows. Beside each window stood a little dark cypress tree. Now he knew why the strange room had seemed so shoddy. It was only meant as scenery. The door on the outside wall was painted on. Between the villa and the stage was a hole. The person who was working the puppets ought to have been down there, but Tonino could only see empty blackness. It was all being done by magic.

Just then he was distracted by a cardboard person diving upwards from the hole, squawking that Mr. Punch had stolen his sausages. Tonino was forced to stand still and squawk back. He was glad of a rest by then. Meanwhile, the cardboard dog seized the sausages and dived out of sight with them. The audience clapped and shouted, “Look at Dog Toby!” The cardboard person sped past Tonino squawking that he would fetch the police.

Once again, Tonino tried to look out at the audience. This time, he could dimly see a brightly lit room and black bulky shapes sitting in chairs, but it was like trying to see something against the sun. His eyes watered. A tear ran down the pink beak on his face. And Tonino could feel that the mean person was delighted to see that. He thought Tonino was crying. Tonino was annoyed, but also rather pleased; it looked as if the person could be fooled by his own mean thoughts. He stared out, in spite of the dazzle, trying to see the mean person, but all he could clearly see was a carving up near the roof of the lighted room. It was the Angel of Caprona, one hand held out in blessing, the other holding the scroll.

Then he was jumped around to face Judy. On the other side of him, the wall had gone from the front of the villa. The scene was the room he knew only too well, with the chandelier artistically alight.

vocabulary.definition: Having or revealing natural creative skill.
vocabulary.example: chandelier artistically alight

vocabulary.definition: (of the eyes) blurred or dim from tiredness.
vocabulary.example: he turned his bleary eyes

vocabulary.definition: To make something unclear or less distinct.
vocabulary.example: it was a blur

vocabulary.definition: Taking up much space; large and unwieldy.
vocabulary.example: black bulky shapes sitting in chairs

translations.ru: резьба по дереву или камню, резной узор

vocabulary.definition: An object or design made by cutting a material such as wood or stone.
vocabulary.example: all he could clearly see was a carving near the roof

vocabulary.definition: An ornamental hanging light with branches for several light bulbs or candles.
vocabulary.example: with the chandelier artistically alight

vocabulary.definition: Quick to understand, learn, and devise or apply ideas; intelligent.
vocabulary.example: What a clever fellow

vocabulary.definition: Aware of and responding to one's surroundings; awake.
vocabulary.example: The mean person wanted him conscious

vocabulary.definition: An evergreen coniferous tree with dark green foliage, often associated with mourning.
vocabulary.example: beside each window stood a little dark cypress tree

vocabulary.definition: A bright, shining, or splendid display that overwhelms the eyes.
vocabulary.example: in spite of the dazzle

vocabulary.definition: Feeling or showing great pleasure.
vocabulary.example: the mean person was delighted to see that

vocabulary.definition: Unable to concentrate because one is preoccupied or worried.
vocabulary.example: he was distracted by a cardboard person

vocabulary.definition: To trick or deceive someone.
vocabulary.example: it looked as if the person could be fooled

vocabulary.definition: Unclear or blurred in appearance or sound.
vocabulary.example: his head and eyes to stop feeling so fuzzy

vocabulary.definition: Illuminated by light.
vocabulary.example: someone from the lighted space

vocabulary.definition: The use of supernatural powers to make things happen or change.
vocabulary.example: It was all being done by magic

vocabulary.definition: Unable to think clearly; confused or dazed.
vocabulary.example: his head still felt muzzy

vocabulary.definition: With little or no delay; immediately.
vocabulary.example: whoever was making him be Mr. Punch promptly took mean pleasure

vocabulary.definition: A cylindrical piece of seasoned minced meat in a casing.
vocabulary.example: whirl the sausages about harder

vocabulary.definition: The natural features of a landscape; in theatre, painted backgrounds or props representing a place.
vocabulary.example: It was only meant as scenery

vocabulary.definition: A roll of parchment or paper often bearing text or decoration.
vocabulary.example: the other holding the scroll

vocabulary.definition: To take hold of suddenly and forcibly.
vocabulary.example: the cardboard dog seized the sausages

vocabulary.definition: Of poor quality or workmanship.
vocabulary.example: the strange room had seemed so shoddy

vocabulary.definition: A drop of clear salty liquid secreted by glands in the eyes as a response to emotion or irritation.
vocabulary.example: A tear ran down the pink beak

vocabulary.definition: To spin or rotate rapidly.
vocabulary.example: whirl the sausages about harder
